skill.name: angular-state-management
skill.description: Guide for choosing and implementing Angular state management with Signals, NgRx, RxJS, and migration patterns.
skill.summary: Implement Angular state management patterns with Signals, NgRx, RxJS, and practical migration guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported hardcoded URLs, command execution patterns, and weak cryptography indicators. Review found no executable scripts, no prompt injection, and no cryptographic implementation; command and weak-crypto matches are false positives from markdown and Angular terminology. The remaining concern is low risk because the skill includes documentation examples with application-relative API calls and public reference links.

low_findings.title: Documentation Examples Include API Request Patterns
low_findings.description: The skill shows Angular fetch and HttpClient examples using application-relative API paths. These examples are not executed by the skill, but copied code should validate identifiers and encode query values before use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network calls are inside markdown TypeScript examples, not runnable skill code. The concern is limited to copy-paste use, especially interpolated id and filter values.

low_findings.title: External Links Are Public Documentation References
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs point to Angular, NgRx, RxAngular, and GitHub documentation. No evidence found of automatic network access, telemetry, credential transfer, or data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are listed as references and resources only. They do not appear in executable code or instructions that send user data externally.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Matches Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: Backtick detections correspond to markdown code fences and inline code labels. No shell, Ruby backtick execution, child process usage, or command invocation was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are fenced markdown examples or inline formatting. Targeted search found no command execution APIs or shell commands.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Matches Are Text False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections matched ordinary documentation and Angular state-management words. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations contain terms such as Signals, Store, filter, and provideStore. Search found no weak cryptographic algorithm names or crypto code.

content.value_statement: Angular teams often struggle to choose between Signals, NgRx, RxJS, and component stores. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical patterns for selecting, implementing, and migrating state management.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use Signals, Signal services, NgRx Store, NgRx SignalStore, and ComponentStore.
Provides TypeScript patterns for local, shared, global, server, URL, and form state.
Shows migration guidance from BehaviorSubject-based services to Angular Signals.
Demonstrates bridging between Signals and RxJS observables with Angular interop helpers.
Includes best-practice tables for derived state, feature colocation, and avoiding over-globalized state.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not inspect or modify a project automatically.
Examples use placeholder services and API paths that require project-specific validation.
It does not cover non-Angular frameworks or backend state design.
It may not reflect future Angular or NgRx API changes after its source date.

use_cases.title: Choose a State Pattern
use_cases.description: Compare Angular state options and select a fit for app size, data flow, and team needs.

use_cases.title: Implement Feature State
use_cases.description: Create practical Signals, SignalStore, NgRx Store, or ComponentStore patterns for a feature.

use_cases.title: Modernize Legacy State
use_cases.description: Plan a migration from BehaviorSubject services to Signals while keeping RxJS integrations clear.

prompt_templates.title: Pick a State Tool
prompt_templates.prompt: I am building an Angular app with [describe app size and state needs]. Recommend the best state management option and explain why.

prompt_templates.title: Design Feature State
prompt_templates.prompt: Design state management for an Angular [feature name] feature using this data flow: [describe inputs, API calls, derived values, and UI states].

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Existing State
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Angular state pattern and propose a migration to Signals or NgRx SignalStore. Preserve behavior and identify risks: [paste relevant code].

prompt_templates.title: Audit Architecture Tradeoffs
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate our Angular state architecture across local, shared, global, server, URL, and form state. Recommend boundaries, stores, effects, and migration order.

output_examples.input: We have a medium Angular app with product filters, loading states, and shared user preferences.
output: A recommendation to keep preferences in a Signal service and product feature state in NgRx SignalStore.
A list of store responsibilities, derived values, loading states, and integration points.
Notes about when to escalate to full NgRx Store.

output_examples.input: We use BehaviorSubject services for user state and want to move to Signals.
output: A migration plan that introduces private writable signals and public read-only values.
Guidance for bridging existing observables during the transition.
Testing and rollout steps for preserving current behavior.

output_examples.input: We need to decide where server state should live in an Angular feature.
output: A comparison of direct HttpClient use, feature stores, and global effects.
Recommendations for loading, error, cache, and optimistic update handling.
Warnings about validating route and query inputs before API calls.

best_practices: Start with the smallest state scope that satisfies the feature requirements.
Use computed values for derived state instead of storing duplicate data.
Define clear bridges when a feature must combine Signals and RxJS observables.
anti_patterns: Do not put every feature state value into a global store by default.
Do not mutate signal-held objects in place without using set or update patterns.
Do not mix Signals and RxJS without a clear ownership boundary.

faq.question: Does this skill run commands or modify files?
faq.answer: No. It is a documentation skill that provides Angular state management guidance and examples.

faq.question: When should I use Angular Signals instead of NgRx?
faq.answer: Use Signals for local or simple shared state. Use NgRx when workflows are complex, cross-feature, or need effects and devtools.

faq.question: Does it cover NgRx SignalStore?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a SignalStore pattern with state, computed values, methods, and feature usage.

faq.question: Can it help migrate BehaviorSubject services?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains how to replace BehaviorSubject services with Signals and how to bridge existing observables.

faq.question: Are the API examples production ready?
faq.answer: They are illustrative. Validate inputs, encode query values, add authentication, and adapt error handling for your app.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
